rationale: Routing + perps push heavy, consistent RPC load across many endpoints; dedicated nodes remove rate-limit exposure during burst and launch windows.
Decoded swap paths and multi-hop transfer enrichment simplify aggregator UX and ops tooling.
Perpetuals matching tolerates no tail latency; Sender guarantees faster, more deterministic transaction landing under volatility.
Raw shred access gives the quant / keeper stack the earliest possible mempool signal.
Dynamic priority-fee guidance improves inclusion rates for keeper flow and liquidations.
Vault rebalancing and lending-position events are high-value webhook surfaces.
Sub-ms account-state streaming is ideal for liquidation-window monitoring.
Liquidation-keeper infrastructure depends directly on low-latency account streams.
Liquidation inclusion during volatility depends on accurate priority-fee positioning.
Marketplace breadth across cNFTs and tokens makes DAS the natural indexing surface.
Listing and transfer notifications power downstream reindexing and pricing jobs.
Complex NFT order flow (bids, pools, compressed) benefits from structured transaction decoding.
Collection metadata, trait indexing, and ownership reads fit the DAS surface directly.
Balances, transfers, and identity surfaces are exactly the shape of Wallet API — consumer-scale ready.
Decoded transaction display is a core wallet UX surface.
Filtered WSS subscriptions support per-wallet real-time activity feeds.
Wallet + exchange surfaces need clean balance/history/identity reads at scale.
xNFT surfaces lean heavily on compressed NFT reads.
Game item economies live on compressed NFTs; DAS is the canonical read surface.
